A narrative document describing the work to be performed by a contractor or project team, including specific deliverables, timelines, location, applicable standards, and acceptance criteria. The SOW is typically incorporated into or attached to a contract.
terms.definitionTR: Proje tarafından teslim edilecek nihai ürün, hizmet veya sonuçların anlatısal (metin tabanlı) bir açıklamasıdır.

terms.definition: The process of selecting only a random portion of a population to measure metrics and verify quality during quality inspection. More efficient than inspecting every item when population is large.
terms.definitionTR: Kalite denetimi sırasında, metrikleri ölçmek ve kaliteyi doğrulamak için ilgilenilen popülasyonun sadece rastgele bir bölümünün seçilmesi işlemidir.

terms.definition: Regularly scheduled communication events to update stakeholders and team members on the project's current status, progress, and performance metrics. Key tool for maintaining stakeholder engagement and transparency.
terms.definitionTR: Projenin mevcut durumunu, ilerlemesini ve performans metriklerini paydaşlara ve takım üyelerine aktarmak için düzenlenen düzenli iletişim etkinlikleridir.

terms.definition: A technique where groups organize user stories and visualize product functionality across a timeline. Especially useful during discovery work for prioritizing the backlog and identifying the minimum viable product.
terms.definitionTR: Özellikle keşif aşamasındaki çalışmalarda, daha büyük grupların kullanıcı hikayelerini organize edip ürünün işlevselliğini görselleştirmesi için kullanılan etkili bir tekniktir.

terms.definition: A relative unit used by agile teams to estimate the size or complexity of a backlog item. Story points capture effort, complexity, and uncertainty as a single measure. Teams use their velocity (points completed per sprint) to forecast when work will be done.
An activity that logically comes after another activity (the predecessor) in a project schedule and depends on it. The nature of the dependency is defined by the relationship type (FS, FF, SS, SF).
terms.definitionTR: Bir zaman çizelgesinde mantıksal olarak başka bir faaliyetten (öncül faaliyetten) sonra gelen ve ona bağımlı olan faaliyettir.

terms.definition: In a project schedule, a grouping of related sub-activities shown as a single comprehensive activity or phase. Used in Gantt charts to provide a high-level view. Also called a 'hammock activity'.
terms.definitionTR: Zaman çizelgesinde, birbiriyle ilişkili birden çok alt faaliyetin toplanarak tek bir kapsamlı faaliyet veya aşama olarak gösterildiği yapıdır.

terms.definition: Money already spent that cannot be recovered. A fundamental principle in decision-making is that sunk costs should not influence future choices — only future costs and benefits should drive decisions. Continuing a failing project simply to justify past spending is a cognitive bias, not rational management.

A strategic analysis framework that identifies an organization's or project's Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats. Used in risk identification, strategic planning, and stakeholder analysis.
terms.definitionTR: Bir kurumun, projenin veya seçeneğin; Güçlü Yönlerini (Strengths), Zayıf Yönlerini (Weaknesses), Fırsatlarını (Opportunities) ve Tehditlerini (Threats) analiz etmeye yarayan risk ve strateji aracıdır.

terms.definition: A team profile with broad general knowledge across multiple disciplines (horizontal bar of the T) and deep expertise in one specific area (vertical bar of the T). Enables flexibility and cross-functional collaboration.
terms.definitionTR: Çevik projelerde tercih edilen, yatay düzlemde birden çok disiplin hakkında genel bilgiye sahipken, dikey düzlemde belirli bir uzmanlık alanında çok derin beceriye sahip olan yetenekli takım profilidir.

terms.definition: Knowledge that is difficult to articulate, archive, and share with others, such as beliefs, personal experience, and practical insights. The opposite of explicit knowledge. Critical for knowledge transfer during project closure.
terms.definitionTR: İnançlar, kişisel deneyimler ve pratik içgörüler gibi kelimelere dökülmesi, arşivlenmesi ve başkalarıyla paylaşılması zor olan bilgi türüdür.

terms.definition: One of the 12 PMBOK® 8th edition principles. Intentionally adapt the project approach, governance, and processes to fit the unique context of each project. No single methodology suits every situation — thoughtful tailoring finds the right balance between rigor and flexibility.

The intentional adaptation of project management approaches, processes, governance, tools, and methods to fit the unique context, culture, complexity, and constraints of a specific project. Effective tailoring avoids both under-management (too little process) and over-management (too much process).

One of the 8 PMBOK® 8th edition performance domains. Addresses activities and responsibilities related to developing, building, and maintaining an effective, motivated, and high-performing project team. Covers leadership, team culture, accountability, and team growth.

The implied cost of rework resulting from shortcuts or quick fixes implemented now instead of proper solutions. Like financial debt, technical debt accrues interest in the form of increasing complexity and maintenance burden over time. Agile teams actively manage technical debt to sustain development velocity.

One of the 12 PMBOK® 8th edition principles. View the project as part of a broader system, recognizing how components interact with each other and with the surrounding environment. Changes in one part of the system create ripple effects that must be anticipated and managed.

An estimating technique that uses three estimates — Optimistic (O), Most Likely (M), and Pessimistic (P) — to calculate a weighted average duration or cost. The PERT formula is: (O + 4M + P) ÷ 6. It accounts for estimation uncertainty better than single-point estimates.

A hybrid contract that combines elements of both fixed-price and cost-reimbursable structures. The buyer pays for actual labor at fixed hourly or daily rates, plus actual material costs. Appropriate when scope cannot be defined in advance but some pricing structure is needed.

A scheduling technique that fixes the duration of an activity or iteration in advance, adjusting scope to fit within that window rather than extending time to complete a fixed scope. Sprints are the most prominent example — the team delivers the best possible outcome within the sprint's fixed length.
terms.definitionTR: Belirli bir hedefe ulaşmak, bir görevi tamamlamak veya bir toplantıyı yapmak için ayrılmış, süresi asla uzatılamayacak, sınırları kesin olarak belirlenmiş maksimum zaman aralığıdır.

terms.definition: The required cost efficiency ratio for remaining work to meet the project's budget goal. TCPI = (BAC − EV) ÷ (BAC − AC). A TCPI above 1.0 means future work must be performed more efficiently than in the past to avoid a cost overrun.

The total amount of time an activity can be delayed from its early start without delaying the project end date or violating a schedule constraint. Activities on the critical path have zero total float — any delay directly delays the project.

A core agile value and servant leadership principle where people openly and honestly share their work in progress, successes, failures, and current status without fear. Enables informed decisions and builds trust.
terms.definitionTR: Özellikle çevik yaklaşımlarda ve hizmetkar liderlikte, insanların ara teslimatlarını, başarılarını, başarısızlıklarını ve mevcut durumu korkusuzca, dürüst ve açık bir şekilde paylaşma kültürüdür.

terms.definition: A model describing the typical progression of team dynamics: Forming (polite orientation), Storming (conflict and competition), Norming (developing cohesion and norms), Performing (high-trust, high-productivity), and Adjourning (dissolution at project end). Teams may cycle back to earlier stages when membership or goals change significantly.
terms.definitionTR: Takım dinamiklerini açıklayan dört aşamalı model: Oluşum (Forming), Fırtına/Karmaşa (Storming), Uyumlanma (Norming) ve Performans (Performing). Bazı versiyonlara Dağılma (Adjourning) aşaması da eklenir.

terms.definition: One of the 8 PMBOK® 8th edition performance domains. Addresses activities and responsibilities for identifying, evaluating, and responding to threats and opportunities arising from uncertainty, ambiguity, and complexity throughout the project life cycle.

A brief, informal description of a product feature from the perspective of a specific user, written in the format: 'As a [user type], I want [capability] so that [benefit].' User stories promote conversation and shared understanding rather than detailed specification documents.

The process of confirming that a deliverable meets the customer's actual needs and expected business value. Answers: 'Did we build the right product?' Distinct from Verification which checks technical compliance.
terms.definitionTR: Teslim edilen ürünün veya hizmetin, müşterinin gerçek ihtiyaçlarını ve beklenen iş değerini karşılayıp karşılamadığının kanıtlanmasıdır. (Doğru ürünü mü yaptık?)

terms.definition: The worth, utility, or importance of something relative to a stakeholder or organization. In PMBOK® 8th edition, delivering value is the purpose of project management — encompassing financial returns, organizational improvements, customer satisfaction, and social or environmental benefits, not merely the completion of tasks.

A lean technique that visualizes all steps in a process from customer request to product delivery to identify bottlenecks, waste (muda), and process gaps. Commonly used in Lean and Kanban environments.
terms.definitionTR: Bir müşteri talebinden ürünün teslimine kadar geçen süreçteki tüm adımları görselleştiren, darboğazları ve israfı (waste) belirlemek için kullanılan yalın (lean) üretim tekniğidir.

terms.definition: A measure that appears to show achievement on the surface but provides no useful information for decision-making or improvement. Example: total page views without context, raw user counts without engagement data.
terms.definitionTR: Görünürde bir miktar sonuç veya başarı gösteren ancak aslında karar vermek veya iyileştirme yapmak için hiçbir faydalı bilgi sağlamayan ölçütlerdir.

terms.definition: A measurable deviation, departure, or difference from a known baseline or expected value. Variances in scope, cost, or schedule trigger corrective or preventive actions.
terms.definitionTR: Projenin bilinen veya onaylanmış temel çizgisinden (beklenen değerinden) ölçülebilir düzeyde uzaklaşması, sapması veya ayrılmasıdır.

terms.definition: A technique for comparing actual project performance to the approved baseline to determine the cause and magnitude of differences in scope, cost, or schedule. Enables informed corrective actions.
terms.definitionTR: Kapsam, maliyet veya zaman çizelgesindeki gerçekleşen performansın, onaylanmış temel çizgiyle karşılaştırılarak aradaki farkların nedenlerini ve etkilerini belirleme tekniğidir.

terms.definition: The projected difference between the total budget and the expected final cost of the project: VAC = BAC − EAC. A positive VAC forecasts an under-budget completion; a negative VAC signals a projected cost overrun.
terms.definitionTR: Projenin başlangıcında belirlenen Tamamlanma Bütçesi (BAC) ile güncel Tamamlama Tahmini (EAC) arasındaki fark olarak ifade edilen beklenen bütçe açığı veya fazlasının net miktarıdır.

terms.definition: The amount of work (typically measured in story points) a scrum team completes per sprint. Tracking velocity over multiple sprints helps teams make realistic commitments, forecast release dates, and identify process improvements. Velocity is a planning tool for the team, not a performance measure for management.

The process of checking through quality control that the product has been produced in accordance with defined technical requirements, specifications, or regulations. Answers: 'Did we build the product right?'
terms.definitionTR: Ürünün, belirlenen teknik gereksinimlere, spesifikasyonlara veya regülasyonlara uygun olarak üretilip üretilmediğinin kalite kontrol süreçleriyle kontrol edilmesidir. (Ürünü doğru mu yaptık?)

terms.definition: A technique used to translate customer requirements into appropriate technical requirements for each project phase, ensuring products truly reflect customer expectations. Captures both stated and unstated needs.
terms.definitionTR: Müşteri gereksinimlerini projenin her fazı için uygun teknik gereksinimlere tercüme ederek müşteri beklentilerini gerçekten yansıtan ürünler sağlamak için kullanılan tekniktir.

terms.definition: Methods used by teams to reach decisions: Unanimity (everyone agrees), Majority (>50% support), Plurality (largest block wins even without majority), and Dictatorship (one person decides).
terms.definitionTR: Takımın kararlara ulaşmak için kullandığı yöntemler: Oybirliği (herkesin aynı fikirde olması), Çoğunluk (%50'den fazla destek), Göreceli Çoğunluk (en büyük bloğun kararı) ve Diktatörlük (tek kişi karar verir).

terms.definition: A companion document to the Work Breakdown Structure that provides detailed information about each WBS component, including scope description, deliverables, assigned resources, cost estimates, schedule milestones, and quality requirements.
terms.definitionTR: İş kırılım yapısındaki her bir iş paketi (bileşen) hakkında ayrıntılı teslimat, faaliyet, zaman çizelgesi ve kabul kriteri bilgileri sağlayan destekleyici belgedir.

terms.definition: The process of evaluating alternative plans to estimate the potential impact of specific scenarios and crises on project objectives. Used to assess project viability under adverse conditions.
terms.definitionTR: Belirli senaryoların ve krizlerin proje hedefleri üzerindeki potansiyel etkilerini tahmin etmek amacıyla alternatif planları değerlendirme sürecidir.

terms.definition: A constraint in Kanban that caps the number of work items permitted in any workflow stage at one time. WIP limits force the team to finish existing work before starting new items, expose bottlenecks, and maintain a consistent flow of value delivery.

A hierarchical decomposition of the total project scope into progressively smaller components, down to work packages that can be scheduled, cost-estimated, monitored, and controlled. The WBS represents 100% of the scope — nothing more, nothing less.
terms.definitionTR: Proje hedeflerini gerçekleştirmek için proje takımı tarafından yürütülecek toplam kapsamın, daha küçük ve yönetilebilir parçalara hiyerarşik olarak ayrıştırılmasıdır.
